posts.content: 2025 LLM Year in Review
posts.highlights: Karpathy reflects on the key developments in LLMs over 2025, likely discussing training against auto-generated data.
posts.worthReading: Provides a high-level summary of LLM progress from one of the field's leading experts.

posts.content:
LLM Knowledge Bases

Something I'm finding very useful recently: using LLMs to build personal knowledge bases for various topics of research interest. In this way, a large fraction of my recent token throughput is going less into manipulating code, and more into manipulating
posts.highlights: Karpathy uses LLMs to construct personal knowledge bases, shifting his focus from code to knowledge management.
posts.worthReading: Illustrates a practical application of LLMs for personal productivity and research.

posts.content: I'm being accused of overhyping the [site everyone heard too much about today already].
posts.highlights: Karpathy responds to criticism about overhyping a popular site, showing self-awareness about hype cycles.
posts.worthReading: Reveals Karpathy's perspective on the balance between excitement and realism in AI.

posts.content: once you attach them to a good coding agent harness at least
posts.highlights: LLMs become more effective when integrated into a robust coding agent framework.
posts.worthReading: Highlights the importance of tooling around LLMs for practical coding tasks.

posts.content: I'm beginning to suspect that a key skill in working effectively with coding agents is developing an intuition for when you don't need to
posts.highlights: Effective use of coding agents requires knowing when to rely on them and when not to.
posts.worthReading: Emphasizes the human skill of judgment in AI-assisted coding.

posts.content: Vibe coding is irresponsibly building software through dice rolls, not caring what code is produced
posts.highlights: Criticizes 'vibe coding' as an irresponsible approach to software development.
posts.worthReading: Warns against over-reliance on AI without proper oversight.

posts.content: This may be the best guidance I've seen anywhere on writing a really good commit history.

posts.content: Visibility is the easiest piece. The hard part is analyzing and understanding what you're observing. I've spoken to teams recording 100k+

posts.content: TL;DR: More and more agents need a workspace: a computer where they can run code, install packages, and access files. Sandboxes provide this

posts.worthReading: Explains the growing need for sandboxed environments for agent execution.

posts.content: Current AI is a librarian of existing knowledge. Science requires an explorer of the unknown.

posts.content: Folks who work in AI or software engineering feel like the world is changing exponential fast.
